name: Nuuanu Valley
desc: Historic valley corridor with grand old estates and temples — lush, cool, and steeped in Hawaiian history.
insider: Old Pali Road (closed to cars) is a hidden walking/biking path through lush forest — the crumbling pavement adds to the atmosphere
Royal Mausoleum (Mauna Ala) is free and holds the remains of Hawaiian royalty including Kamehameha II through V
Nuuanu Stream floods fast in heavy rain — the 1960s flood devastated lower Nuuanu and the risk remains real
The Japanese temples and shrines along Nuuanu Ave are stunning — Soto Mission and Izumo Taishakyo are open to visitors
Pali Lookout is the famous viewpoint but the real secret is the abandoned Old Pali Road trail below it
Queen Emma Summer Palace on Pali Highway is a charming small museum with gorgeous grounds — $6 admission
The Battle of Nuuanu in 1795 was fought here — Kamehameha I drove rival warriors off the Pali cliffs
Property ranges wildly — modest cottages near the stream to grand estates on the valley walls above $5M
Nuuanu YMCA and the community pool are local family hubs — very affordable and welcoming
Mosquitoes are aggressive in the lower valley, especially near the stream — bug spray is essential year-round
